name: forge
description: Agent-safe database schema migration analyzer. Detects risk, data loss, and lock time for PostgreSQL migrations before they execute. Zero false negatives on data-loss operations.

requires: forge binary in PATH or forge/ directory
invocation.bridge_note: Zero v0.1.x lacks world.in (stdin reading). mcp-bridge.sh manages the JSON-RPC session; forge binary handles schema analysis. When Zero adds stdin support (V34), this bridge will be replaced by a native Zero MCP server.

signing.manifest_sha256: UNSIGNED in source — cosign sign-blob runs in CI (release.yml) at publish time
signing.sig_file: forge/mcp-manifest.json.sig (produced by CI, uploaded as release artifact)

tools.name: forge_analyze
tools.title: Analyze Schema Migration
tools.description: Analyze migration risk between two database schemas. Returns structured JSON with risk tier (SAFE/NOTABLE/MEDIUM/HIGH/CRITICAL), per-operation risk scores, data loss flags, estimated lock times, and agent guidance. Always run this before executing a migration. Not a migration executor — read-only analysis only, makes no schema changes. Not for non-PostgreSQL schemas or input not in TABLE/COLUMN format.

from_schema.description: Current (before) schema in Forge Schema Format. Lines: TABLE <name> or COLUMN <name> <type> [NOT_NULL] [NULLABLE] [PRIMARY_KEY] [DEFAULT]. Separate lines with newlines.

to_schema.description: Desired (after) schema in Forge Schema Format. Same format as from_schema.

request_id.description: Optional idempotency key. ASCII printable only (no quotes or backslashes). If provided, echoed in response for log deduplication.

human_acknowledgment_token.description: Nonce from a prior approval_nonce response. Pass after obtaining human sign-off on a decision_required migration. Expires 5 min after issue. Single-use.

decision_required.description: If true, agent MUST NOT auto-proceed. Surface to human principal before executing migration.

approval_nonce.description: Present when decision_required: true and no token was provided. Pass as human_acknowledgment_token after human sign-off. Expires in 5 minutes.

approval_prompt.description: Human-readable instruction to surface to the principal when decision_required: true.

proceed_ok.description: True when a valid human_acknowledgment_token was verified. Agent may proceed with the migration.

_rate_limit.description: Present on all successful responses. Monitor remaining to avoid RATE_LIMITED.

INVALID_INPUT.fix: Check schema content: no pipe characters (|), valid TABLE/COLUMN format, max 64 KiB per schema

INVALID_SCHEMA.fix: Schema parsing failed. Verify TABLE/COLUMN line format in docs.

EXEC_FAILED.fix: forge binary produced no output. Verify binary is present and executable.

UNKNOWN_TOOL.fix: Tool name not recognized. Use forge_analyze.

RATE_LIMITED.fix: Wait retry_after_seconds from error body. Monitor _rate_limit.remaining in prior responses.

INVALID_ACKNOWLEDGMENT_TOKEN.fix: Re-run forge_analyze (no token) to obtain a fresh approval_nonce. Tokens expire after 5 minutes and are single-use.

rate_limits.note: forge_analyze is stateless (no registered org). Both per-IP and per-org limits apply. IP limit is primary for unauthenticated callers.

rate_limit_spec.state: in-memory bridge process (resets on bridge restart)

security.content_policy: Tool descriptions audited against mcp-security-spec.md §4.3. No instruction-format language. ≤ 512 chars.

agent_quickstart: 1. Run `forge --describe` to get the full schema of commands and formats.
3. If decision_required is true, surface the analysis and approval_nonce to a human principal.
4. After human approves, call forge_analyze again with human_acknowledgment_token: <nonce>.
5. Proceed only when the response includes proceed_ok: true.
6. If has_data_loss is true, treat as decision_required regardless of risk_tier.
7. Check each operation's agent_guidance for specific retry and safe-execution advice.
